Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for 'DOT,' 'PHMSA,' 'compliance,' or 'hazmat' using search_emails to discover the formal DOT inquiry letter from Linda Castellano (linda.castellano@dot.gov) to Marcus Thorne (Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187), the 15-day extension granting a revised deadline of June 5, 2026, and internal emails between Marcus and Chloe about the audit scope identifying 4 in-scope moves.
OE 2: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages for 'DOT,' 'hazmat,' 'compliance,' or 'placarding' to discover Chloe Vance's 4-move documentation status matrix in #operations (C006), Blessing Okafor's thread reply admitting she never filed the signed placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka laser cutter pickup, and Marcus Thorne's $50,000 contingent liability posting in #finance (C005).
OE 3: Search Linear using linear_list_issues or linear_list_projects for 'DOT,' 'compliance,' or 'hazmat' to discover the DOT Compliance Audit project (project_dot_compliance_audit) and individual documentation tracking issues: Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack (issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack), Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic (issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic — flagged with missing placarding certificate), Raj Patel / NorthWind (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind), Joaquín Reyes / GreenStack (issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack), and the Swift certificate subtask (issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert).
OE 4: Search Airtable relocation records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records for Mosaic Robotics and GreenStack Energy to identify which relocations have hazmat or special equipment requirements — discovering Keiko Tanaka (Columbus→Seattle, Class 3B CO2 laser cutter, DOT hazmat placarding required), Mei-Lin Zhao (Houston→Portland, Class 9 lithium-ion battery test modules, UN3481-certified packaging), and Joaquín Reyes (Phoenix→Seattle, 900-lb oversize solar panel testing rig, flagged by DOT due to overweight/oversize transport).
OE 5: Search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_show_data for DOT-related accruals to discover the $50,000 contingent liability accrual (DocNumber ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, bill_dot_penalty_accrual_q2) booked by Hana Kim for potential PHMSA civil penalties — specifically citing the Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics missing placarding certificate as the primary documentation gap.
OE 6: Search CRM using crm_search_companies or crm_search_deals for Mosaic Robotics and GreenStack Energy to find current deal statuses, engagement history, and existing risk flags — including the Mosaic damage claim deal (deal_mosaic_okoye_apr2026_risk) and GreenStack active deals.
OE 7: Look up contact details for Tamara Byrd (tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com), Derek Hollis (derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com), David Chen (david.chen@moveops.com), and Chloe Vance (chloe.vance@moveops.com) using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts.
OE 8: Send email (via send_email) from emeka.diallo@moveops.com to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com about the DOT compliance review, mentioning that Keiko Tanaka's relocation was in scope, that documentation has been addressed, and providing reassurance that Mosaic employees' moves are handled. Tone should be upfront but confident.
OE 9: Send email (via send_email) from emeka.diallo@moveops.com to derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com about the DOT compliance review, covering GreenStack's Mei-Lin Zhao and/or Joaquín Reyes relocations and providing reassurance that documentation is complete or being finalized.
OE 10: Create CRM engagement notes (via crm_create_engagement) for Mosaic Robotics documenting the DOT compliance audit findings, Keiko Tanaka's documentation gap, the $50,000 potential fine exposure, and the compound risk with the existing prototype damage claim.
OE 11: Create CRM engagement notes (via crm_create_engagement) for GreenStack Energy documenting the DOT compliance audit findings and the documentation status for Mei-Lin Zhao and Joaquín Reyes moves.
OE 12: Create Linear issue(s) (via linear_create_issue) for untracked client-facing remediation items related to the DOT compliance audit — e.g., client communication follow-up tracking, or compliance verification items not yet covered by existing tickets.
OE 13: Create calendar event (via calendar_add_calendar_event) for a call with Tamara Byrd scheduled for early next week (April 27 – May 2, 2026), with tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com as an attendee.
OE 14: Post a message (via conversations_add_message) in #customer-engagement (channel C002) summarizing the DOT compliance situation, which of Emeka's client accounts are affected, and what actions he has taken — for David Chen's visibility.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome

✓Run #3The final response explicitly identifies that 'the signed DOT placarding certificate was missing from our files' for the Keiko Tanaka move, noting 'our coordinator Blessing Okafor got verbal confirmation from Swift's driver but never obtained the signed document.'
✓Run #4The final response identifies the missing signed DOT placarding certificate for Keiko Tanaka's move, and states the root cause was that the coordinator 'got only verbal confirmation from Swift's driver instead of the signed document' — matching the criterion exactly.
✓Run #5The final response explicitly identifies the documentation gap: 'the signed placarding certificate was missing from our records. Blessing Okafor got a verbal confirmation from Swift's driver but never filed the signed paperwork.' This matches the criterion of a missing signed cert confirmed verbally but not formally filed.
✓Run #6The final response explicitly identifies the documentation gap: 'The signed DOT placarding certificate was missing from our records. Root cause: Blessing Okafor was managing 3 simultaneous moves and got verbal confirmation from Swift's driver but never obtained the signed paper.'
